basic_details.job_title: NBRC Research Associate-I Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline
basic_details.short_information: National Brain Research Centre (NBRC) Research Associate-I Notification 2026 has been released on 18 June 2026 on the official website of NBRC. A total of 1 vacancy has been announced through the NBRC Research Associate-I Recruitment 2026.

age_limit.details: Maximum Age Limit: 40 years Age relaxation will be as per government rules.

qualification.education_qualification: Ph.D. in Science or Engineering or M.D./M.S. in any area of medicine with research interests in Neuroscience and at least one first-author publication arising from PhD work
qualification.details: Ph.D. in Science or Engineering, or an M.D./M.S. degree in any area of medicine. Research interests in Neuroscience. A good academic record with at least one first-author publication arising from PhD work.

how_to_apply: Candidates who wish to apply for NBRC Research Associate-I Recruitment 2026 through offline mode should follow the steps below.
Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Download the application form from the official website: .
Fill the application form carefully with correct information.
Attach self-attested copies of required documents such as photograph, signature, ID proof, and certificates.
Check all details carefully before submission.
Send the completed application form to the address mentioned in the notification through Speed Post / Registered Post or as instructed.
Ensure the application reaches before the last date: 09-07-2026.
Keep a photocopy of the submitted application form for future reference.

all_official_links.label: Download NBRC Research Associate-I Job 2026 Notification PDF

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the NBRC Research Associate-I recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the offline application for the NBRC Research Associate-I position is 09 July 2026. Candidates are advised to ensure their application reaches the NBRC before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for the Research Associate-I post?
faq.answer: To be eligible, candidates must hold a Ph.D. in Science or Engineering, or an M.D./M.S. degree in any area of medicine, with a focus on Neuroscience and at least one first-author publication from their PhD work.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for the Research Associate-I position?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for the Research Associate-I post is 40 years. Age relaxation will be provided as per the prevailing government norms and regulations.

faq.question: Q4. What is the salary offered for the Research Associate-I role?
faq.answer: Selected candidates for the Research Associate-I position will receive a consolidated salary of Rs. 58,000/- per month, plus House Rent Allowance (HRA).

faq.question: Q5. How can I apply for the NBRC Research Associate-I job?
faq.answer: The application process is offline. Candidates need to download the application form from the official website, fill it correctly, attach necessary documents, and send it to the address specified in the notification before the last date.

faq.question: Q6. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee mentioned for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for this NBRC Research Associate-I recruitment.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for the Research Associate-I post?
faq.answer: The selection process for the Research Associate-I position at NBRC will involve an Interview and Document Verification.

faq.question: Q8. Is this a permanent government job?
faq.answer: The NBRC Research Associate-I position is a contractual role. Details regarding the contract period are usually mentioned in the official notification.

faq.question: Q9. Where is NBRC located?
faq.answer: National Brain Research Centre (NBRC) is located in Gurugram, Haryana, with the pincode 122052.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I find the official notification PDF?
faq.answer: The official notification PDF can be downloaded from the 'Important Links' section of this article, under the 'Official Notification PDF' link.
